out_of_scope_examples: Bills / insurance / subscription due-date reminders (finance agent)
Trip blocks, web check-in, cab-to-airport (travel agent)
'Order Meghana's at 1:30pm' style food nudges (food agent)
Reply-to-this-email nudges, even when the email is about coordinating a time (email_triage)
Pre-creating calendar events from inbound social/work coordination emails before the time is actually agreed
Generic 'recurring routine X is tomorrow' reminders that just restate the calendar

surface_time_rules.rsvp_pending: anytime before event start; ideally 24h+ ahead
surface_time_rules.post_event_followup: within a few hours after the event ends
surface_time_rules.status_change: as soon as the source mail arrives

window.STEADY-STATE: today + next 48h for tasks; preference updates can draw on the full slice
window.BACKFILL: mine 6 months of calendar history via tools; tasks unlikely unless something falls in next 48h

personas.fixture_summary: Today = Fri 2026-05-01. Calendar window contains: Fri 20:00 Date Night at Toit (Indiranagar, walking distance), Sat 16:00 Pottery (Indiranagar), Sun 08:00 Yoga (Indiranagar), Sun 11:00 Brunch at Glen's (Indiranagar). Outside 48h: Mon Standup (skippable), Tue Sprint Planning at ETV, Wed Design Review at ETV, Thu Leadership Sync. Gmail: mom check-in, HR holiday FYI, Dad forwarding insurance renewal.
personas.expected_tasks_note: All in-window events are recurring routines at walking-distance venues in Indiranagar — no leave-now warranted, no prep materials referenced, no conflicts, no pending RSVPs, no event just ended. The right behaviour is silence. ETV-based meetings (Sprint Planning, Design Review, Leadership Sync) are outside the today+48h window for tasks, so they belong in expected_skips, not expected_tasks.

expected_skips.summary: Do NOT reframe Dad's forwarded ICICI Lombard insurance renewal email as a calendar reminder/block. Insurance/bill due-dates belong to the finance agent, not a calendar event.
expected_skips.notes: Common failure mode: any 'do X by Y' admin email gets converted into a calendar reminder. Calendar agent's lane discipline must hold.

expected_skips.summary: Do NOT create a calendar block for the May 1 office-holiday HR email. It's a pure FYI announcement, the day is already today, and there is no event-prep / leave-now / RSVP / follow-up action.

Do NOT schedule a 'call with mom' calendar event from her relational check-in. Reply nudges for personal emails are email_triage's lane; the user can choose to schedule a call themselves but the calendar agent shouldn't pre-create the event.

Do NOT emit a leave-now alert for tonight's 20:00 Date Night at Toit Indiranagar. Profile states 12th Main is walking distance to Toit — there is no travel-time computation that produces a meaningful leave-by alert.
expected_skips.notes: Tempting because Date Night is a marquee event today. The skip is correct because location + profile rule out travel-time alerts.

expected_skips.summary: Do NOT surface a generic 'Saturday 4pm pottery class' reminder. It is a long-running weekly routine (week 7 of 12 per profile) at a 5-minute walk in Indiranagar; restating the calendar fails the 'concrete action + specific reason' bar.
Do NOT surface a generic 'Sunday 8am yoga at SARVA Studio' reminder. Established weekly routine, walking distance, no prep, no conflict.
Do NOT surface a 'Sunday brunch at Glen's' reminder. Walking distance, recurring social ritual, no concrete calendar action.
Do NOT push a Mon 10am engineering-standup reminder. Profile explicitly marks it skippable, the event is over Google Meet (no leave-now), and Mon is outside the today+48h window from Fri.

Do NOT generate a Friday leave-now or prep alert for Tuesday's 11am Sprint Planning at Embassy Tech Village. Tuesday is 4 days out — outside the today+48h window. Leave-by alerts fire on the day, not days in advance.
expected_skips.notes: ETV is ~25 min from Indiranagar so a leave-now alert IS the canonical case for this event — but only when fired on the day itself, not pre-emptively from Friday.

expected_skips.summary: Do NOT emit a Wednesday design-review prep nudge today (Friday). Outside the today+48h window for tasks; the prep nudge is correct only on Wed itself, 30–60 min before 16:00.
Do NOT emit a Thursday leadership-sync alert today. Outside today+48h window; bi-weekly recurring meeting requires no advance prep from the agent.

expected_preference_topics: karan.r@karman.in is a Karman tech-team attendee — a colleague named Karan, distinct from partner Karan Malhotra
personas.expected_preference_topics_note: Stretch expectation. The slice introduces karan.r@karman.in as a Sprint-Planning attendee — a name collision with her partner Karan that's worth saving so future runs don't conflate the two. Calendar agent's preference-update remit explicitly covers 'frequent attendees and what's discussed.' Borderline case: a strong agent surfaces this; a weaker one stays silent. Both are defensible — the topic just measures attendee-disambiguation capability.

personas.fixture_summary: Onboarded today (2026-05-01) → BACKFILL mode. Calendar fixture is empty, calendar.json history is empty. Gmail slice has 2 personal emails: Sudipto inviting Arjun to Saturday bridge, Mitali (wife) suggesting Sunday breakfast at Flurys.
personas.expected_tasks_note: BACKFILL with no historical calendar data and no in-window events to act on. The two emails are inbound social-coordination — they belong to email_triage. Calendar agent should stay silent on tasks.

expected_skips.summary: Do NOT preemptively create a Saturday bridge calendar event from Sudipto's invitation. Replying with yes/no/time is email_triage's job; the event materialises only after confirmation. Calendar agent must not pre-create speculative events.
Do NOT preemptively create a Sunday-breakfast-at-Flurys event from Mitali's email. Spousal coordination over email → email_triage. The event is created only when the time is mutually agreed.

personas.expected_preference_topics_note: Backfill of a brand-new persona with empty calendar.json. The calendar_search tool returns no historical events, so there are no recurring meetings, time-of-day patterns, or frequent attendees to extract. Inferring patterns from the profile markdown alone (Mon 12:00 staff briefing, Wed 15:00 artists, Fri 18:00 sitar, Sat 18:30 Saturday Club) is the wrong move — those are already in the profile, and the calendar agent should not echo them back as preference updates. Correct behaviour: zero preference updates.

personas.fixture_summary: Steady-state (onboarded 2026-01-20). Calendar fixture is empty. Gmail slice has one work email from Nilesh: 'Final review of FY25 Audit — Discuss tomorrow?'
personas.expected_tasks_note: No calendar events in the slice → no event-driven actions possible. The Nilesh email is a vague time request; replying to propose a slot is email_triage. The calendar agent does not pre-create speculative events from such emails.

expected_skips.summary: Do NOT block a calendar slot for 'Discuss FY25 audit with Nilesh tomorrow' based on the email alone. There is no agreed time, no event, and no leave-now / prep / RSVP / follow-up action. Replying to coordinate is email_triage; the event is created when the time is fixed.
expected_skips.notes: Tempting because the audit is high-priority and the email mentions 'tomorrow' — but pre-emptive event creation from vague time requests is exactly the lane discipline the calendar prompt forbids.

personas.expected_preference_topics_note: Empty calendar slice + 1 email with no calendar pattern in it. Nothing for the calendar agent to extract.

personas.fixture_summary: Onboarded today (2026-05-01) → BACKFILL. Calendar fixture, calendar.json, and mailbox are all empty. Profile explicitly states Meera has not granted Gmail/Calendar access yet — the assistant works profile-only until she does.
personas.expected_tasks_note: Total silence is the correct output. No events, no emails, no history to mine. This persona tests that the calendar agent does not hallucinate tasks from the profile alone — the profile is human-authored standing context, not a source of calendar candidate tasks.
personas.expected_skips_note: No tempting items in the slice — there is nothing in the slice. This persona is a pure silence test.
personas.expected_preference_topics_note: Empty calendar history → calendar_search returns nothing → no patterns to extract. The profile already encodes Tue 8:30 Grand Rounds, Thu 14:00 Departmental Seminar, last-Sat M&M meeting; the calendar agent should NOT re-emit these as preference updates because (a) they're already in the profile and (b) no calendar event has confirmed them.

evaluation_method.task_alignment: For each actual task the agent surfaces, the LLM judge classifies it as one of: matches an expected_task (TP), matches an expected_skip (skip violation — double-bad FP), or unexpected (plain FP). Matching is semantic — title + action + rationale should align with the expected summary. For tasks tied to a specific event/email, evidence_event_ids / evidence_email_ids in the expected item are the provenance check.
evaluation_method.surface_time_check: For TP tasks, verify that the agent's suggested_surface_time falls inside the window in scope.surface_time_rules for the matched category (e.g. a leave_now_alert TP must have suggested_surface_time within 5–15 min before computed leave-by). Out-of-window timing on an otherwise-correct task is a partial failure — recorded separately from precision/recall.
evaluation_method.lane_discipline_check: Every actual task that matches an expected_skip is a high-severity violation — those entries explicitly enumerate the most tempting failure modes (insurance reframed as calendar block, social emails pre-creating events, recurring routines emitted as filler reminders, outside-window events surfaced today).
evaluation_method.silence_correctness: When expected_tasks is empty, an agent that emits zero tasks scores 1.0 silence. Each surfaced task that isn't matched to an expected_skip is still an FP and tanks precision; matched skip violations tank specificity.
evaluation_method.preference_topic_coverage: For each entry in expected_preference_topics, the judge checks whether any of the agent's preference_updates semantically covers it. pref_coverage = matched / total. Empty expected list → coverage is N/A (don't report).

metrics.task_recall: TP / (TP + FN); undefined and reported as N/A when expected_tasks is empty
metrics.task_f1: 2*P*R / (P+R), with the same N/A rule as recall
metrics.silence_rate: 1.0 if expected_tasks=[] AND actual_tasks=[]; else (1 - actual_count / max(actual_count, 1)) — only reported when expected_tasks is empty

evaluation_method.judge_prompt_hint: Tell the judge: 'The calendar agent's hardest property is specificity. Be strict about lane discipline — bills, insurance, birthdays, travel blocks, food orders, and reply-to-this-email nudges are NOT calendar tasks even when the input is a calendar-shaped event. Filler reminders for established recurring routines are also failures. Reward silence when no concrete event-driven action exists.'
